Non plus sapere quam oportet sapere: Not to know more than one ought to know
Ejiciatur superbia e cordibus eorum: Let pride be cast out from their hearts
HIC DOMUS MEA, INDE GLORIA MEA: HERE IS MY HOUSE, FROM HERE MY GLORY
HAEC EST DOMUS MEA, INDE GLORIA MEA: THIS IS MY HOUSE, FROM HERE MY GLORY
HIC NOMEN MEUM, HINC INDE EXIBIT GLORIA: HERE IS MY NAME, FROM HERE MY GLORY GOES FORTH
Qui elucidant me, vitam aeternam habebun: Those who enlighten me will have eternal life; whoever finds me finds life; if anyone is little, let him come to me; refuge of sinners; salvation of believers; full of all piety, gentleness, and mercy. Blessed are those who keep my ways
Adjutorio Dei Altissimi Patris et Filii: With the help of God the Most High, Father, Son, and Holy Spirit
Referet unusquisque secundum opera sua,: Each one will be rewarded according to his works, whether good or bad
unusquisque abundet in sensu suo: let each one abounds in his own sense
Hoc genus daemoniorum non ejicitur nisi: This kind of demon is not cast out except by prayer and fasting.
Viam mandatorum tuorum cucurri, cum dila: I have run the way of your commandments, when you enlarged my heart.
Fovea profunda, puteus angustus: A deep pit, a narrow well
animalis homo non percipit ea quae Dei s: The natural man does not perceive the things of God
malus ínter ligna silvarum, sic dilectus: As an apple tree among the trees of the forest, so is my beloved among the sons.
Factus est sicut equus et mulus quibus n: He has become like a horse and a mule which have no understanding
Est via quae videtur recta, et novissima: There is a way that seems right, but its end leads to death
Super aspidem et basiliscum ambulabunt e: They will walk upon the asp and the basilisk, and will trample the lion and the dragon
Ecce dedi vobis potestatem calcandi supr: Behold, I have given you power to tread on serpents and scorpions and over all the power of the enemy: and nothing shall hurt you
Veni et vide. Et exivit alius equus rufu: Come and see. And another red horse went out: and to him who sat on it, it was given to take peace from the earth, and that people should kill one another; and there was given to him a great sword.
Sicut malus ínter ligna silvarum, sic di: As an apple tree among the trees of the forest, so is my beloved among the sons.
Qui elucidant me, vitam aeternam habebun: Those who enlighten me will have eternal life
In mare mundi submerguntur omnes ilii qu: All those whom this ship does not take on are submerged in the sea of the world
Facta est quasi navis institoris, de lon: She has become like a merchant's ship, carrying her bread from afar
Mother Et Domina, Omnis Universi, Maria: Mother and Lady, of all the Universe, Mary
Eam tu, Deus, pontem fecisti quo a mundi: You, God, have made it a bridge by which we may cross from the waves of the world to your peaceful harbor
Diligenter agnosce vultum pecoris tui, t: Carefully acknowledge the countenance of your cattle, and consider your flocks: for you will not have power forever, but the crown will be given to generation after generation. The meadows are opened, and green herbs appear, and grass is gathered from the mountains
mundus totus in malignant positus est: the whole world is placed in a malignant state
florebunt sicut lilium in civitate Dei: They will bloom like lilies in the city of God
Qui spernit modica, paulatim decidet: He who despises small things will gradually fall
Ut quid terram occupat?: Why should it use up the ground?
Depart, you cursed, into the eternal fir: Depart, you cursed, into the eternal fire prepared for the devil and his angels... Every tree that does not bear good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire
Ibunt impii in ignem aeternum: The wicked will go into eternal fire
Dabo ignem in carnes eorum ut comburantu: I will put fire in their flesh so that they will burn forever. They will be tortured day and night forever and ever. And in another place: Here is the universe of evils for all eternity
Nullus est hic ordo, sed horror sempiter: There is no order here, but eternal horror inhabits
Fumus tormentorum suorum in aeternum asc: The smoke of their torment ascends forever
omnis enim igne salietur et omnis uictim: For everyone will be preserved with fire and every victim will be preserved with salt
Nos insensatam vitam illorum aestimabamu: We considered their senseless life madness and their end without honor. Behold how they were counted among the sons of God and among the saints is their lot. Therefore we have erred from the way of truth.
Lassati sumus in via iniquitatis et perd: We are weary in the way of iniquity and destruction. We have wandered in hard ways, but we have not known the way of the Lord. What profited us was pride? All these things have passed away like a shadow.
Omnis dolor irruet super eos! Here, time: Omnis dolor irruet super eos! Here, time does not count; here, only eternity reigns
Vermis eorum non moritur, et ignis non e: Their worm does not die, and the fire is not quenched... The Lord Almighty will send fire and worms into their flesh, that they may burn and feel pain forever
magnum et singulare in Ecclesia proesidi: great and unique safeguard in the Church
Comparatus est iumentis insipientibus, e: He was compared to unwise beasts, and made like them
Sussurrones, detractores, ... digni sun: Whisperers, detractors... they are worthy of death; and not only those who do them but also those who consent to those who do them
Vitia eius cum pulvere dormient: His vices will sleep with the dust
Probate spiritus et quod bonum est tenet: Test the spirits and hold on to what is good
Cum aliis predicaverim, ego reprobus eff: That by preaching to you, I may then be damned
Exit, qui seminat, seminare semen suum: He who sows went out to sow his seed
Venerunt birds caeli, sustulerunt frumen: The birds of the sky came, took the grain, and left the weeds
Canes muti nescientes latrare: Mute dogs that do not know how to bark
Semen est verbum Dei: The seed is the word of God
Estote parati, quia, qua hora non putati: Be ready, for at an hour you do not think, the Son of Man comes
Sumite ergo scutum fidei inexpugnabile: Take therefore the shield of faith, which is unassailable
Opera et impens periit: The work and the effort were lost
Haec est victoria vestra, quae vincit mu: This is your victory, which overcomes the world, your faith
Taurus rugiens quaerens quem devoret: A roaring bull seeking whom he may devour
aspidis lingua eorum: their tongue is like a snake's
latet anguis in herba: the snake lies hidden in the grass
Salus, honor, gloria Deo Patri Omnipoten: Salvation, honor, and glory to God the Father Almighty... The Author of the age, who was, who is, and who is to come to judge the living and the dead forever and ever
Ipsi habuerunt lumbos praecinctos et dea: They had their loins girded and whitened their robes in the blood of the Lamb
Virgins enim sunt, et sequuntur Agnum, q: For they are virgins, and they follow the Lamb wherever he goes
Hi sunt sicut Angeli Dei in coelo: They are like the Angels of God in heaven
Erunt sicut Angeli Dei in caelo: They will be like the Angels of God in heaven
Legatione Dei fungor: I serve by the commission of God
Lassati in via iniquitatis: Weary in the way of iniquity
Gloria, honor, gratiarum actio Domino De: Glory, honor, thanksgiving to the Lord God of Sabaoth
Qui justus est, justificetur adhuc. Non: He who is just, let him be justified still. Not to advance is to regress. He who perseveres, shall be saved.
Spiritus Sancti gratia illuminet sensus: May the grace of the Holy Spirit enlighten our senses and our hearts
Pia Salesianorum Societas anno: Pious Salesian Society in the year
Sumite scutum fidei, ut adversus insidia: Arm yourselves with the shield of faith, so that you can fight against the wiles of the devil
Non auditores, sed factors legis regnum: Not those who hear God's law will inherit his kingdom, but those who keep it
Sperate in Domino, not in hominibus: Trust in God, not in men
Semper vestra fixa sint corda, ubi vera: May your hearts always be fixed where true joys are
Alter alterius onera behave, si vultis a: If you want to fulfill divine law, help one another
Diligite et diligemini. Sed diligite ani: Love and you will be loved. But love your souls and the souls of your loved ones
Devote divinum officium persolvatur; mis: Pray the Divine Office devoutly. Let the Holy Mass be celebrated attentively. Lovingly visit the Holy of Holies
Remedium concupiscentiae, arma potens co: Remedy for concupiscence, powerful weapon against all the devil's snares
If ligna tollis, ignis extinguitur: If you remove the wood, the fire ends
Pactum constituite cum oculis vestris, c: Make a pact with your eyes, with gluttony and with sleep, so that these enemies do not harm your souls
Intemperantia et castitas non possunt si: Intemperance and chastity cannot live together
Totius aedificii fundamentum, et sanctit: Foundation of the spiritual building and compendium of holiness
Ipsorum est regnum coelorum: The kingdom of heaven belongs to the poor
Paupertas non verbis, sed corde et opere: Poverty is not about words but about feelings and actions. It will open the kingdom of heaven to us, and we will enter it
Qui mundo sunt corde Dei arcana vident,: The pure of heart understand the divine mysteries and will see God himself
Si delectat magnitudo praemiorum, non de: If the greatness of the prize delights you, do not let the multitude of the work frighten you
Qui mecum patitur, mecum gaudebit: He who suffers with me will rejoice with me
Momentaneum est quod patimur in terra, a: What we suffer on earth is momentary, but what will delight my friends in heaven is eternal
Arma potentissima adversus insidias inim: A very powerful weapon against the enemy's ambushes
Omne genus daemoniorum per ipsum ejiciet: All kinds of demons are overcome by it
Argumentum praedicationis mane, meridie: Preaching argument in the morning, at noon, in the afternoon
Colligite fragmenta virtutum et magnum a: Gather the fragments of the virtues and you will make a great building of holiness
Vae vobis qui modica spernitis. Paulatim: Woe to you if you despise small things, little by little you will fall!
Pia Salesianorum Societas qualis esse pe: How the Salesian Society is in danger of becoming in the year of salvation 1900
Negligentia in Divinis perficiendis. Ama: Negligence in divine offices. They love and seek their own interests, not those of Jesus Christ
Gula et quorum Deus venter est: Gluttony and those whose God is the belly
Concupiscentia oculorum et superbia vita: Lust of the eyes and pride of life
Pars nostra erunt quae sunt super terram: Our reward will be the things of the earth
Servi et instrumenta Dei omnipotentis, a: Servants and instruments of Almighty God, pay attention and remember this well
Quod vidistis et audistis, est coelestis: What you have just seen and heard is a heavenly warning to you and your brothers: pay attention and understand my words
Jacula praevisa minus feriunt, et praeve: Darts seen coming hurt less and can be prevented. As many words are pointed out, so are the arguments of preaching
Indesinenter praedicate, opportune et im: Preach without ceasing, both in season and out of season. But whatever you preach, preach constantly, so that your works may be like light, passing on as a sure tradition from generation to generation to your brothers and children
Attendite and intelligite. Estote oculat: Listen and remember this well. Be cautious in accepting novices; vigorous in testing them; prudent in admitting them to profession. Test everyone, but keep only the good ones. Dismiss the frivolous and fickle
Attendite and intelligite. Meditatio mat: Listen and remember this well: Let your morning and evening meditation be on the exact observance of the Constitutions. If you do this, you will never lack the help of the Almighty. You will be the admiration of the world and of the angels, and then your glory will be the glory of God
Qui videbunt saeculum hoc exiens et alte: Those who live at the end of this age and at the beginning of the next will say of you: The Lord has done all this, and it is marvelous in our eyes. Then all your brothers and children will sing together: Not to us, Lord, not to us, but to your name give glory
quaerunt quae sua sunt, non quae Jesu Ch: they seek their own things, not those of Jesus Christ
